← the Atlas

Sleeping terminals

Features· seedling ·accepted ·JUN 20, 2026

A plan for Sleep/Wake. Model the terminal as a sum — Terminal = active or sleeping — so a dormant terminal is a STATE on one record, not a separate thing. Presence (canvas, dock, minimap) reads the union; touching a live field (PTY, agent) narrows to active. Phased — seat the sum (zero behavior change), then sleep/wake, then frozen screenshot, then unify with session-restore.

Contents

  • A terminal is active or sleeping
  • Presence reads the union, liveness narrows
  • The phases
    • Phase 1 — seat the sum (zero behavior change)
    • Phase 2 — Sleep / Wake
  • Trade-offs & when we’d revisit

You asked for Sleep: leave a Claude Code terminal blocked for days, sleep it (its PTY, xterm, WebGL context, and agent all released — gone, like closing it), and wake it later in place with the agent resumed.

The model is one move: make the terminal a sum — Terminal = active | sleeping. A dormant terminal is a state on the same record, so it inherits focus, drag, dock ordering, and persistence for free — it is the terminal the canvas already renders, just without a PTY. Four phased PRs; the first is a zero-behavior-change foundation.

A terminal is active or sleeping

The fold already lives in the schema. surface.ts splits a terminal’s fields into a persisted base (cwd · git · intent · theme · canvasLayout — survives a restart) and a live overlay (agent · foreground · live-PR · the PTY/xterm/attach handles — “never persisted; a restore must re-derive it”). That partition is active-vs-sleeping, so the sum maps onto bases that already exist — exactly one field is added:

const ActiveTerminalSchema =
  PersistedTerminalFieldsSchema.merge(LiveTerminalFieldsSchema)   // live overlay present
    .extend({ state: z.literal("active") });

const SleepingTerminalSchema =
  PersistedTerminalFieldsSchema                                   // base only — overlay absent by type
    .extend({ state: z.literal("sleeping"), sleptAt: z.number() });

const TerminalMetadataSchema =                                   // the wire / collection shape
  z.discriminatedUnion("state", [ActiveTerminalSchema, SleepingTerminalSchema]);

type Terminal =
  | ({ state: "active" }                    & PersistedTerminalFields & LiveTerminalFields)
  | ({ state: "sleeping"; sleptAt: number } & PersistedTerminalFields);

// Presence reads the union; touching a live field MUST narrow.
const placeTile  = (t: Terminal) => t.canvasLayout;             // both arms — no narrow
const routeInput = (t: Terminal) => {
  if (t.state !== "active") return;                             // compiler-forced narrow
  send(t /* now carries pr · agent · foreground + a live PTY */);
};

sleptAt is the sleeping arm’s analogue of the live overlay — the only new scalar. An active terminal is base + overlay; a sleeping terminal is base + sleptAt. Sleeping is one record whose state flag says whether its PTY is currently spawned.

Presence reads the union, liveness narrows

Putting the discriminant on the terminal buys two structural properties:

  • Type-safe presence vs liveness. A consumer that reads agent/foreground/pr/PTY/xterm must first narrow state === "active"; the compiler refuses a live field on the bare union. There is no live-only list to read from by mistake — so a presence surface (canvas, dock, minimap, arrange, cycle, switcher) physically cannot omit or mis-render a sleeping terminal. Input routing resolves inside the active narrow, so a sleeping terminal can be the active/selected/panned-to tile yet is never an input target. holdsWebgl/webglTileBudget filter on the active set, so a sleeping terminal holds no WebGL context by construction.
  • One persistence channel. SavedTerminal is already PersistedTerminalFields + id — the sleeping arm’s exact payload minus sleptAt. A restored terminal and a slept terminal are the same on-disk record, distinguished only by state. Session save emits one list; sleeping terminals join it; restore is bulk-wake, wake is restore-one, sleep is the inverse.

The phases

Each is one reviewable PR; each leaves master shippable.

PhaseWhat landsWhy separable
1 — Seat the sumAdd state; flip TerminalMetadataSchema to a discriminatedUnion; presence surfaces read the Terminal union off the terminal store; ship with only the active arm constructedZero behavior change — a pure structural move that makes the narrowing seam exist before any sleep logic depends on it
2 — Sleep / WakePopulate the sleeping arm: sleep snapshots the base + releases PTY/xterm/agent; wake respawns from the base; sleeping terminals join the canvas/dock as ordinary cards and rowsThe user-facing core; no new component, no second loop
3 — Frozen screenshot bodyCapture just before sleep, write under KOLU_STATE_DIR, serve through a small static image route; the reference rides the recordIsolated surface — one capture + one route
4 — Unify with session-restoreCollapse boot-restore and wake onto one seam (rehydrate a persisted terminal from its base); the session snapshot and sleeping records become one ordered setBehavior-preserving consolidation, landed last

Phase 1 — seat the sum (zero behavior change)

Shipped — #1449 . The server registry holds only the active arm (TerminalProcess.meta: ActiveTerminal); the union flows on the client, where presence surfaces narrow state === "active" through one activeArm seam.

Add the state discriminant to the terminal and flip TerminalMetadataSchema from a flat .merge to z.discriminatedUnion("state", …), but construct only the active arm. Every terminal is active, so the UX is pixel-identical; what the phase delivers is the state === "active" seam, compiling and in place. Presence surfaces — the canvas <For>, dock ranking, the minimap, arrange-by-repo, the nextTerminal/prevTerminal cycle, view-posture’s count gate, the right-panel hasTerminals check — read the Terminal union directly off the terminal store, with no registry between them. The canvas card shell, the layout writer, and arrange’s clustering algorithm are untouched; only their data source changes.

Two things land here because the schema flip forces them:

  • The migration. SavedTerminal is persisted, so the discriminatedUnion rejects a legacy record with no state key. A state.ts migration stamps state: "active" on every legacy terminal and bumps SCHEMA_VERSION — the one sanctioned place to supply the default. Read sites narrow; they never coalesce (a read-time ?? "active" is a defect — fail fast).
  • The liveness-reader sweep. Every reader that touches agent/foreground/pr/PTY/xterm (the agent indicator, the PR popover, the xterm pane, the WebGL budget, input routing, activeMeta) either stays in the presence subset or adds a state === "active" narrow. That sweep is the bulk of the diff — and it is the win: afterward a live field on a bare terminal won’t compile.

Acceptance: master is shippable, the UX is identical, and the seam every later phase leans on is in place.

Phase 2 — Sleep / Wake

Populate the sleeping arm. Sleep snapshots the persisted base, then releases the PTY/xterm/agent — persist before kill, never kill-then-pray, so a crash mid-sleep loses nothing. Wake respawns from the base and resumes the agent. A terminal transitions active → sleeping atomically on one record, so there is no “live wins” dedup window and no cross-arm transition to reconcile. Sleeping terminals join the one set the canvas and dock already iterate, so they are focusable/draggable/resizable cards and selectable dock rows with no new component and no second loop — only a dormant placeholder body (Phase 3 swaps in the frozen frame) and one new verb, Wake. On boot they rehydrate as sleeping, never auto-woken, and count toward tileCount so a sleeping-only workspace stays on the canvas.

Non-negotiables for Phase 2

These are requirements, not nice-to-haves — each closes a failure mode that is easy to ship without noticing.

  • Snapshot the top terminal only. A sleeping record is a single terminal — splits are released, not frozen. The record is rooted by construction (its id is the terminal it was), so no orphan record can arise.
  • Tolerate a malformed record; never let one poison the set. Validate shape in the schema and enforce the cross-field invariant (the base is well-formed) by dropping a bad record at the read boundary — never a fatal validator on the persisted collection. The stakes are high because sleeping records share the terminal list: one corrupt record must not break the whole list. (See the persisted-schema-stays-tolerant code-police rule.)
  • First-class and visually distinct in every presence surface. A sleeping terminal renders — moonlit, ☾, dimmed — in the canvas, dock, minimap, switcher, and mobile; arrange clusters it with its repo; the cycle traverses it. The sum makes omission unrepresentable, but verify it on each surface.
  • A sleeping tile can be the active tile. Click/select focuses it; input routing narrows to active, so it is never an input target — a type fact, not a runtime guard.
  • An e2e that drives the real failure modes. Seed a malformed record (it must not poison the set), sleep a terminal (it must not vanish), wake it (it must be usable), and assert sleeping appears on each presence surface — driven like a user, not asserted on counts. A green count-only test over a fresh state proves nothing here.

Why the order is the point

Sleep’s machinery is small. The design work is where a dormant terminal lives — Phase 1 answers that with a flag instead of a layer, and everything after is a body, a route, or a consolidation. Small, because the structure finally fits the thing.

Trade-offs & when we’d revisit

  • A migration is mandatory. SavedTerminal is persisted, so adding state needs the state.ts migration above. There is no shipping this as a pure refactor.
  • A wide type-level ripple is the diff. Flipping TerminalMetadataSchema to a discriminatedUnion forces every liveness reader to either stay in the presence subset or add a narrow. That sweep is the win, but it is the bulk of the work.
  • state must be lifecycle-owned. Only the sleep/wake transitions write state; a metadata provider must never resurrect a live field on a sleeping record (the overlay isn’t on that arm — a type error, which is the point).

When we'd revisit

If a genuine non-terminal canvas kind ever lands — a sticky note, a web embed — introduce a content-kind layer then, designed against two real kinds. Location is structure: the receptacle is justified the day there’s a second thing to put in it, and not before. (See electricity.mdx — the same trap, named three ways.)
